skill.name: sales-enablement
skill.description: When the user wants to create sales collateral, pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling docs, or demo scripts. Also use when the user mentions 'sales deck,' 'pitch deck,' 'one-pager,' 'leave-behind,' 'objection handling,' 'deal-specific ROI analysis,' 'demo script,' 'talk track,' 'sales playbook,' 'proposal template,' 'buyer persona card,' 'help my sales team,' 'sales materials,' or 'what should I give my sales reps.' Use this for any document or asset that helps a sales team close deals. For competitor comparison pages and battle cards, see competitor-alternatives. For marketing website copy, see copywriting. For cold outreach emails, see cold-email.
skill.summary: Create professional sales collateral including pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling guides, demo scripts, and ROI calculators
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 65 potential issues, primarily 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns that are false positives from Markdown code blocks containing template examples. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are template placeholders in one-pager examples (lines showing `[Logo]`, `[Tagline]`, etc.). The 'with statement' finding is a Python keyword used in documentation context. These are documentation/markdown files with instructional content, not executable code. No malicious intent, no network activity, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempts detected. All findings are contextually benign.

low_findings.title: Template Placeholders in Documentation
low_findings.description: The one-pager-templates.md file contains template placeholders using bracket notation (e.g., `[Logo]`, `[Tagline]`, `[CTA Button/Link]`) which were flagged by static analysis as potential Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns. These are documentation examples showing layout structure for sales collateral templates, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct examination of the file confirms these are markdown template examples with bracketed placeholders, not code execution patterns. The context is clearly instructional documentation for sales materials.

low_findings.title: Python Keyword in Documentation
low_findings.description: Line 225 in SKILL.md contains the word 'with' which was flagged as a deprecated 'with statement'. This is plain English text in a sentence about document formatting, not Python code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms this is English prose describing document deliverables, not Python code. The 'with' keyword appears in natural language context.

content.user_title: Create Professional Sales Collateral in Minutes
content.value_statement: Stop wasting time rewriting sales materials. Generate pitch decks, one-pagers, and objection handling docs that your sales team will actually use. Built on proven B2B sales frameworks.
actual_capabilities: Generate slide-by-slide sales deck outlines with speaker notes using proven storytelling frameworks
Create scannable one-pagers and leave-behinds tailored to specific buyer personas and use cases
Build objection handling libraries with structured response approaches for common sales objections
Design demo scripts and talk tracks for discovery calls, technical deep-dives, and executive briefings
Create ROI calculators with input fields, calculation formulas, and value visualization
Develop sales playbooks, buyer persona cards, and proposal templates with customization guidance
limitations: Requires you to provide product-specific context, value propositions, and differentiators
Templates need customization with your brand voice, visual style, and specific product details
Cannot conduct customer research or gather proof points like metrics and quotes from your data
Output is text-based structure and content guidance, not visual design or formatted documents

use_cases.title: Launch New Sales Collateral
use_cases.description: Product marketers and sales enablement managers creating pitch decks, one-pagers, and battle cards for new product launches or go-to-market initiatives.

use_cases.title: Equip Sales Reps for Objection Handling
use_cases.description: Sales leaders building objection handling libraries and talk tracks to help reps respond confidently to price, timing, and competitor objections.

use_cases.title: Customize Materials for Specific Deals
use_cases.description: Account executives creating tailored one-pagers, ROI analysis, and demo scripts for strategic prospects or enterprise deals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Sales Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a sales deck for [PRODUCT DESCRIPTION]. We sell to [TARGET PERSONA] at [COMPANY SIZE/TYPE]. Our main differentiators are [LIST 3 DIFFERENTIATORS]. Apply the 10-12 slide framework and include speaker notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a pitch deck from scratch for a new product or refreshing an existing deck with better storytelling

prompt_templates.title: Build Objection Handling Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Our top objections are: [LIST OBJECTIONS]. Create an objection handling document with structured responses for each. Include the real concern behind the objection, a response approach, proof points, and follow-up questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Arming sales reps with responses to the most common objections they hear on calls

prompt_templates.title: Generate One-Pager Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-pager for [USE CASE/PERSONA]. Our product helps [TARGET AUDIENCE] [ACHIEVE OUTCOME]. Key differentiators: [3 POINTS]. Include layout guidance and make it scannable.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a leave-behind document for post-meeting follow-ups or trade show handouts

prompt_templates.title: Design ROI Calculator
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an ROI calculator structure for [PRODUCT]. Inputs should include: [LIST METRICS]. Show how to calculate: [TIME SAVINGS/COST REDUCTION/REVENUE IMPACT]. Output should display ROI percentage and payback period.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a tool to demonstrate quantifiable value during sales conversations

output_examples.input: Help me create a sales deck for our HR analytics platform. We sell to HR directors at mid-market companies (500-5000 employees). Our differentiator is predictive AI that identifies flight risk before employees quit.
output_examples.output: A 12-slide deck outline including: 1) The Hidden Cost of Turnover, 2) Why Traditional Retention Tools Fail, 3) The Shift to Predictive Analytics, 4) Our Approach: Intervene Before the Decision, 5) Product: Flight Risk Dashboard, 6) Product: Intervention Recommendations, 7) Proof: 40% Reduction in Voluntary Attrition, 8) Case Study: How TechCo Cut Turnover in Half, 9) Implementation: Live in 4 Weeks, 10) ROI: $2.4M Saved Annually for 500-Employee Company, 11) Pricing: Tiered by Employee Count, 12) Next Steps: Pilot Program Rollout. Each slide includes key messaging and speaker notes tailored to HR director priorities (retention metrics, budget impact, implementation ease).

output_examples.input: We need objection handling for: 'too expensive,' 'we don't have budget this quarter,' and 'we can build this internally.'
output_examples.output: Objection handling library with three entries: 1) 'Too expensive' - Response framework acknowledging budget concerns, reframing around ROI (payback period example), offering pilot to prove value, follow-up question about cost of inaction. 2) 'No budget this quarter' - Response addressing timing, offering flexible payment, suggesting smaller starting package, bridging to next fiscal year planning. 3) 'Can build internally' - Response acknowledging build-vs-buy complexity, highlighting hidden costs (maintenance, updates, opportunity cost), proof points from companies who switched back from custom tools, follow-up about core vs. context priorities. Each objection includes 2-3 response variations for different conversation contexts.

best_practices: Always check for product-marketing-context.md first to understand existing positioning before creating new collateral
Tailor content to specific buyer personas (technical, economic, champion) rather than creating generic one-size-fits-all materials
Focus on business outcomes and ROI rather than feature lists - every claim should tie to revenue, efficiency, or risk reduction
Keep documents scannable with clear visual hierarchy, short bullets, and bold headers so sales reps can find information in seconds during live calls
anti_patterns: Don't create comprehensive feature dumps - sales reps need situation-specific guidance, not encyclopedic product catalogs
Avoid marketing language in sales collateral - use the words and phrases your sales team actually uses on calls, not polished brand messaging
Never bury the next step - every asset should end with a clear, time-bound call-to-action for both the rep and the prospect

faq.question: What types of sales collateral can this skill create?
faq.answer: This skill generates sales decks/pitch decks, one-pagers and leave-behinds, objection handling libraries, demo scripts and talk tracks, ROI calculators, sales playbooks, buyer persona cards, proposal templates, and case study briefs. Each asset type uses proven B2B frameworks and includes structure, content guidance, and customization notes.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my own product information?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill provides frameworks and structure, but you need to supply product-specific details: what you sell, who it's for, your differentiators, pricing, proof points (customer metrics, quotes), and target personas. The more context you provide, the more tailored and useful the output will be.

faq.question: Can this skill create visual designs or formatted documents?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates text-based content including slide outlines, document structure, copy, and layout guidance. You will need to apply visual design, formatting, and branding using tools like PowerPoint, Google Slides, Figma, or your preferred design software. The skill can guide visual hierarchy and design principles but cannot create visual assets.

faq.question: How is this different from copywriting or competitor-alternatives skills?
faq.answer: Sales-enablement focuses on internal sales collateral used by reps to close deals (decks, one-pagers, objection docs). Copywriting handles external marketing content like website pages and emails. Competitor-alternatives creates public-facing comparison pages. Use sales-enablement for assets your sales team uses directly in conversations and proposals.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates for different industries or buyer types?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The skill includes guidance on tailoring content by buyer persona (technical buyer vs. economic buyer vs. champion), industry vertical, company size, and deal stage. Specify your target audience and use case, and the skill will customize messaging, proof points, and emphasis accordingly.

faq.question: What if I already have product marketing context documented?
faq.answer: This skill automatically checks for .agents/product-marketing-context.md (or .claude/product-marketing-context.md in older setups) before starting. If that file exists, it reads the context and only asks for information not already covered or specific to your current task. This prevents repetitive questions and ensures consistency across your sales materials.
